name: Kesava Misra
dates_notes: Keśava Miśra is usually placed around the thirteenth century by the reception of the Tarka-Bhāṣā before the fully developed Navya-Nyāya curriculum.
core_thesis: Keśava Miśra is the Nyāya author of the Tarka-Bhāṣā, a teaching manual that presents the older Nyāya system in a compact form before Navya-Nyāya's fully technical idiom dominates the curriculum. Its importance is introductory and transitional: it gives students definitions of pramāṇa, inference, debate categories, fallacies, and realist ontology in a manageable prose format. The clean GRETIL file titled Tarkabhāṣā inspected in this run is a Buddhist text, so this entry does not mark Keśava's Tarka-Bhāṣā as present in the corpus.

engaged_works.title: Tarka-Bhasa
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely attributed to Keśava Miśra in the Nyāya pedagogical tradition.
engaged_works.summary: A concise Nyāya manual. The Tarka-Bhāṣā presents the school's epistemology and logic in teachable prose: perception, inference, comparison, testimony, the structure of inference, fallacies, debate, and the realist categories inherited from Nyāya-Vaiśeṣika. It is important because it represents a pre- or early-Navya curricular form of Nyāya, standing between the older sūtra-commentary stack and the later technical literature of Gaṅgeśa and his successors.
